flashcards.Back: The maximum groundspeed for a small unmanned aircraft system (sUAS) is \(\text{87 knots}\) or \(\text{100 mph}\), as defined by Part 107 regulations.
The maximum altitude a sUAS can be flown is \(\text{400 feet}\) above ground level (AGL), or higher if flying within a \(\text{400-foot}\) radius of a structure.
According to Part 107, the minimum visibility required to operate a sUAS is \(\text{3 statute miles}\) from your control station.
The remote pilot in command must inspect the sUAS to ensure it is in a condition for safe operation, per regulations.
You cannot operate a sUAS if you have consumed alcohol within the preceding \(\text{8 hours}\), or if your blood alcohol content is \(\text{0.04}\) or greater.
A remote pilot cannot operate an sUAS in a manner that interferes with operations and traffic patterns at any airport, heliport, or seaplane base per Part 107.
Operating a sUAS over people is prohibited unless you have a waiver or the operation meets the requirements of Category \(\text{1, 2, 3, or 4}\).
sUAS operations are allowed during daylight hours or during civil twilight with appropriate anti-collision lighting that is visible for \(\text{3 statute miles}\).

flashcards.Front: Reporting an accident to the FAA
flashcards.Back: An accident must be reported to the FAA if it involves serious injury to any person or loss of consciousness, or if the sUAS sustains substantial damage. Notification is required within \(10\) days.
A Certificate of Waiver (CoW) is required to deviate from certain provisions of Part 107, such as nighttime operations or flying beyond visual line of sight (BVLOS).

flashcards.Front: Remote pilot in command (remote PIC) responsibilities
flashcards.Back: The Remote PIC is responsible for ensuring the sUAS is safe to fly and that the operation is conducted in accordance with Part 107 regulations.
To operate in Class B airspace, you typically need authorization from Air Traffic Control (ATC) via LAANC or a manual waiver, ensuring safe integration.
Operations in Class C airspace require ATC authorization. Utilize LAANC when available, or manually request permissions when necessary.
Similar to Class C, operating in Class D airspace also requires ATC authorization before commencing sUAS operations near airports.

flashcards.Front: Operating near airports without control towers
flashcards.Back: When operating near airports without an operating control tower, you should self-announce your intentions on the Common Traffic Advisory Frequency (CTAF).
Temporary Flight Restrictions (TFRs) are issued to restrict aircraft operations in certain areas temporarily. Remote pilots must check for TFRs before each flight to ensure compliance.
Remote pilots must establish lost link procedures. These include predetermined actions the sUAS will take in the event of a lost link, such as returning to a pre-programmed location.
The Remote PIC is responsible for ensuring that the sUAS receives proper maintenance per manufacturer's guidelines and Part 107 requirements.
The FAA Remote Pilot Certificate is valid for \(\text{24 calendar months}\). Recurrent testing or training is required to maintain currency.
You must have your Remote Pilot Certificate and registration readily available during all sUAS operations as per FAA regulations.
While not explicitly mandated in Part 107, using supplemental weather data can improve decision-making for a safer flight operation.
Part 107 restricts carriage of hazardous materials with few exceptions for items necessary for the safe operation of the sUAS.
Night operations require completion of updated FAA training and using anti-collision lights visible for \(\text{3 statute miles}\) from the sUAS.
If you require corrective lenses, you must have them available during flight operations per regulations on pilot medical certificates.
A sUAS cannot be operated from a moving aircraft or vehicle unless over a sparsely populated area; regulations apply per Part 107.
Communicate effectively with ATC, providing accurate location and altitude information, and follow instructions precisely for safe airspace integration.
Remote pilots may deviate from Part 107 in response to an in-flight emergency, as long as the FAA is notified upon request.
The VO should maintain VLOS with the sUAS, scan the airspace for hazards, and communicate effectively with the remote pilot in command.
During operations, ensure adequate longitudinal separation, as outlined in FAA guidelines, to avoid potential collisions with other aircraft.
When near airports, be aware of the airport's layout, traffic patterns, and potential wake turbulence from larger aircraft, adhering to safety guidelines.
The maximum groundspeed for a small unmanned aircraft system (sUAS) is \(87\) knots or \(100\) mph. This limit ensures safe operation and control of the drone.

flashcards.Front: Maximum altitude AGL for suas operation
flashcards.Back: The maximum altitude for sUAS operation is \(400\) feet above ground level (AGL) or, if higher, within \(400\) feet of a structure. This is per 14 CFR Part 107.

flashcards.Front: Minimum visibility requirement for suas flight
flashcards.Back: The minimum visibility requirement for sUAS operations is \(3\) statute miles (SM) from the control station. This ensures adequate visual awareness of the surrounding airspace.

flashcards.Front: Definition of visual line of sight (VLOS)
flashcards.Back: VLOS means the remote pilot or visual observer must be able to see the unmanned aircraft throughout the entire flight, unaided by any device other than corrective lenses.
The remote pilot in command (PIC) must conduct a preflight inspection to ensure the sUAS is in a safe condition for operation. This includes checking control links.
The remote pilot must have their remote pilot certificate and registration available for inspection upon request by the FAA or law enforcement during sUAS operations.
Operations at night are not allowed without a waiver, even with anti-collision lights. Part \(107.29\) defines requirements for night operations with a waiver.

flashcards.Front: Maximum blood alcohol content for remote pilot
flashcards.Back: The maximum blood alcohol content (BAC) for a remote pilot is \(.04\). Remote pilots are also prohibited from operating under the influence of drugs.

flashcards.Front: Weather minimums for operating near clouds
flashcards.Back: When operating near clouds, you need to maintain a distance of \(500\) feet below the cloud and \(2,000\) feet horizontally. This ensures safe operation and airspace awareness.
A control station is an interface used by the remote pilot to control the flight path of the small unmanned aircraft. The remote pilot directly manipulates it.
Part \(107\) applies to civil small UAS operations conducted for compensation or hire. This includes commercial, educational, and research operations.

flashcards.Front: What is the weight limit for suas?
flashcards.Back: A small unmanned aircraft system (sUAS) must weigh less than \(55\) pounds, including payload, to be operated under Part \(107\) regulations without further restrictions.
sUAS operations from a moving vehicle are not allowed unless over a sparsely populated area or water and no property is being transported for compensation.
Operations in Class B, C, D, and E airspace require prior authorization from Air Traffic Control (ATC). Apply through LAANC or DroneZone for permissions.
Operating an sUAS in a careless or reckless manner that endangers the life or property of another person is prohibited under Part \(107\) regulations.

flashcards.Front: Who is responsible for avoiding collisions?
flashcards.Back: The remote pilot in command (PIC) is directly responsible for avoiding collisions with other aircraft, people, and objects on the ground during sUAS operations.
All sUAS operated under Part \(107\) must be registered with the FAA, and the registration number must be displayed on the aircraft per \(14\) CFR Part \(48\).
To deviate from certain Part \(107\) regulations, a remote pilot must apply for a waiver through the FAA's DroneZone website. The FAA reviews each waiver request.
A 'small unmanned aircraft' is an unmanned aircraft weighing less than \(55\) pounds, including everything that is onboard or otherwise attached to the aircraft.
A remote pilot certificate is valid for \(24\) calendar months. You must pass a recurrent knowledge test to renew it before expiration to continue operating commercially.

Hazardous material is defined as any substance or material that has been determined by the Secretary of Transportation to be capable of posing an unreasonable risk to health, safety, and property when transported in commerce.
According to Part \(107\), operation over people is prohibited unless you have a waiver or your drone meets specific operational requirements for Category \(1\), \(2\), \(3\), or \(4\) operations.
When operating within the lateral boundaries of an airport, the maximum allowable altitude is determined by the Airport Elevation and any FAA restrictions in place.
'Operational control' refers to having the authority to initiate, conduct, continue, divert, or terminate a flight. The remote PIC always holds operational control.

flashcards.Front: Minimum age for remote pilot certificate
flashcards.Back: The minimum age requirement to obtain a remote pilot certificate with a small UAS rating is \(16\) years old. You must also be able to read, speak, write, and understand English.

flashcards.Front: Suas carriage of property for hire
flashcards.Back: Carriage of property of another for compensation or hire is allowed under Part 107, provided it meets specific requirements including operation within VLOS and weight limits.
